images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of the expert upcycling procedure.Step 1:Pre-train anEE-expert MoE forτ\tausteps.Step 2:Apply the upcycling operator𝒰m\mathcal{U}_{m}at stepτ\tau: each experteeis replicatedre≥1r_{e}\geq 1times (high-utility experts receive more copies,rE>ri≥⋯≥r1r_{E}>r_{i}\geq\cdots\geq r_{1}, s.t.∑re=m⋅E\sum r_{e}=m\cdot E), and the router is extended with replicated slots plus bias noise. All copies are identical atτ\tau, providing a warm initialization.Step 3:Continue pre-training on the expandedm​EmE-expert model forT−τT{-}\tausteps; stochastic gradient diversity breaks symmetry among duplicates, driving specialization. Top-KKrouting is fixed throughout, so active parameters and per-token compute are unchanged.

Figure 2:Expert upcycling at 50% CPT on the 7B→\to13B interleaved MoE.Left:Upcycled (32→\to64) requires 27,888 GPU hours, saving 32% over Fixed-64 (41,328 hours) while using 32% more than Fixed-32 (21,168 hours).Center:Validation loss of Upcycled (1.305) is lower than Fixed-32 (1.339) and close to Fixed-64 (1.308).Right:Downstream benchmark accuracy on six representative tasks; Upcycled matches or exceeds Fixed-64 on HellaSwag, PIQA, OpenBookQA, and Social IQA, while closing most of the gap on MMLU and ARC-Challenge.
